back_translation: If someone sins by failing to testify when he hears a public oath about something he has witnessed, whether he has seen or known of it, and he does not report it, he shall bear his iniquity.
Or if a person touches any unclean thing—whether the carcass of an unclean wild animal or unclean livestock or unclean crawling creature—even if he is unaware, he is unclean and guilty.
Or if he touches human uncleanness—anything by which one can become unclean—even if he is unaware, when he realizes it, he is guilty.

Or if someone swears thoughtlessly with his lips to do anything good or evil—in whatever a man may rashly pronounce in an oath—even if he is unaware, when he realizes it, he is guilty in any of these.
If someone incurs guilt in one of these ways, he must confess the sin he has committed.

And he must bring his guilt offering to the Lord for the sin he has committed: a female lamb or female young goat from the flock of small animals as a sin offering. And the priest will make atonement for him concerning his sin.

And if his hand cannot afford a lamb, he shall bring his guilt offering for the sin he has committed: two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the Lord—one as a sin offering and one as a burnt offering.
He shall bring them to the priest, who shall first present the one for the sin offering. He shall twist its head at the front of its neck without separating it.
And he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar, while the rest of the blood is drained at the base of the altar. It is a sin offering.
And he shall prepare the second bird as a burnt offering according to the ordinance. And the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin he has committed, and he shall be forgiven.

But if his hand cannot reach two turtledoves or two young pigeons, he shall bring his offering for the sin he has committed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a sin offering. He must not put oil on it, and he must not place frankincense on it, because it is a sin offering.

And he shall bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take a full handful from it as its memorial portion and burn it as smoke on the altar atop the offerings by fire to the Lord; it is a sin offering.
And the priest shall make atonement for him for any of these sins he has committed, and he shall be forgiven. The remainder shall belong to the priest, like the grain offering.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,

If someone acts unfaithfully and sins unintentionally against the holy things of the Lord, he must bring his guilt offering to the Lord: an unblemished ram from the flock of small animals at proper valuation in silver shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, as a guilt offering.

Regarding what he has sinned against the holy thing, he shall make restitution, and he shall add a fifth of its value to it, and give it to the priest, and the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering, and he shall be forgiven.
And if someone sins and violates any of the Lord's commandments which is forbidden to be done, and is unaware, he is guilty and shall bear his iniquity.

He shall bring an unblemished ram from the flock of small animals at proper valuation as a guilt offering to the priest. And the priest shall make atonement for him for his unintentional wrong which he committed and was unaware of, and he shall be forgiven.
It is a guilt offering; he was certainly guilty before the Lord.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
If someone sins and acts unfaithfully against the Lord by deceiving his neighbor in regard to a deposit, or property entrusted to his hand, or by robbery, or if he oppresses his neighbor—
Or finds lost property and lies about it and swears falsely—in any of the things a man may do to sin in regard to them—
When he sins and becomes guilty, he must return what he stole, or what he gained by oppression, or the deposit entrusted to him, or the lost property he found,
Or anything about which he swore falsely. He shall make restitution in full and add a fifth of its value to it; he shall give it to the owner on the day of his guilt.
And he shall bring his guilt offering to the Lord: an unblemished ram from the flock of small animals at proper valuation, as a guilt offering, to the priest.
And the priest shall make atonement for him before the face of the Lord, and he shall be forgiven for any of the things he did to incur guilt.
